description: Improve E2E test assertion quality and add missing test coverage: better assertion diagnostics in analyze-site tests, add tests for browser_get_websocket_frames tool, and add positive test for extension_get_side_panel.

userStories.title: Improve analyze-site test assertions for better failure diagnostics
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want analyze-site E2E test failures to show the actual data instead of 'expected false to be true' so that debugging is faster.
acceptanceCriteria: Suggestion presence checks use expect(array).toContainEqual(expect.objectContaining({ toolName: '...' })) instead of expect(array.some(...)).toBe(true)
At least 5 assertion sites in analyze-site.e2e.ts are updated with this pattern
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/analyze-site.e2e.ts

Throughout the file, suggestion presence checks use this pattern:
```
expect(result.suggestions.some(s => s.toolName === 'get_profile')).toBe(true);
```

This produces a useless failure message: 'expected false to be true'. Replace with:
```
expect(result.suggestions).toContainEqual(expect.objectContaining({ toolName: 'get_profile' }));
```

This shows the actual suggestions array on failure, making it clear which suggestions were generated vs expected.

Search for all instances of `.some(s =>` followed by `.toBe(true)` and replace with the `toContainEqual` pattern. There are at least 10-15 instances across the file in tests for cookie-session, JWT, GraphQL, JSON-RPC, API key, tRPC, WebSocket, mixed auth, SPA, and Basic Auth scenarios.

Note: Do NOT change the `auth.types` assertions that use `toContain` — those check string arrays and `toContain` is correct for them.

userStories.title: Add E2E test for browser_get_websocket_frames tool
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want E2E test coverage for the browser_get_websocket_frames tool so that regressions in WebSocket frame capture are caught.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test in browser-tools.e2e.ts exercises browser_get_websocket_frames
The test opens a page that establishes a WebSocket connection, enables network capture, and verifies captured frames
The test handles the case where WebSocket frame capture may not be available (debugger API limitation) gracefully
userStories.notes:
File: e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts

The `browser_get_websocket_frames` tool is listed in BROWSER_TOOL_NAMES (e2e/helpers.ts:233) but has zero E2E test coverage. The analyze-site-test-server.ts has a WebSocket scenario at /websocket-app/ that creates a WebSocket server (using the 'ws' package) which could be reused as the test target.

Approach:
1. Add a new test.describe block after the 'Browser tools -- network capture lifecycle' block (~line 1729)
2. The test should:
   a. Open a tab to a URL that establishes a WebSocket connection (could use the analyze-site test server's /websocket-app/ endpoint, or add a /ws endpoint to the main test-server.ts)
   b. Call browser_enable_network_capture on the tab
   c. Wait briefly for the page's WebSocket to connect and exchange messages
   d. Call browser_get_websocket_frames with the tabId
   e. Verify the result contains frame entries (or handle gracefully if the feature is not supported)

Note: The analyze-site test server already has a WebSocket server at e2e/analyze-site-test-server.ts. To use it, you'd need to start it via the fixture. Alternatively, it may be simpler to add a basic WebSocket endpoint to the existing test-server.ts. Check what's easiest.

Another option is to use the existing test server and open the /websocket-app/ scenario from analyze-site-test-server. But since browser-tools tests use the standard testServer fixture, the simplest approach might be to add a WebSocket endpoint to test-server.ts or to use a data: URL page that connects to an echo WebSocket.

userStories.title: Add positive test for extension_get_side_panel when panel is open
userStories.description: As a platform contributor, I want a test that verifies extension_get_side_panel returns { open: true } when the side panel is actually open so that the tool is verified in both states.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test verifies extension_get_side_panel returns open: true when the side panel page is open
The test opens the side panel, calls extension_get_side_panel, and asserts open is true
The existing negative test (open: false when not open) is preserved
